repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade2: common-restriction LP/SVAR support equality when both sides impose the same contact set

published_prior_art.cite: plagborgmoller_wolf_2021_lp_var: LPs and VARs estimate the same population impulse responses under common identifying restrictions

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade: three-channel mechanism factorization and iff frontier using sign, pasting, and cumulant channels
eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade: reduction to the parent cumulant-envelope margin

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: Verified metadata disagree with the bibliography for at least Wolf 2022, Drautzburg-Wright 2023, and Hoesch-Lee-Mesters 2024.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The support equality under identical common restrictions is already the PMW common-estimand result plus equality of optimization domains.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The same common-restriction equality is already present in eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade2.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship focal object c_w(P) is not computed in §9; Exhibit 9.1 computes only one comparison quotient, not the infimum over K_w(P) or Gamma_w(P), so tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship gap cites Read 2022, Drautzburg-Wright 2023, and the non-Gaussian SVAR papers without a location-level anchor tying them to the specific iff frontier gap.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The parent-margin collapse is already the advertised repair target in eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade and is not an independent novel theorem.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumptions compact and curvature make c_w(P)>0 and Gamma_w(P)>0 effectively built into the hypotheses, while the claimed equality case requires a zero channel that Assumption compact excludes.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 uses theta=pi/4 as the common comparison even though Delta_kappa(pi/4)=1, so that rotation is infeasible for Q_C where Delta_kappa=0; the displayed loss is not h_H-h_C.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem assumes Assumption compact, which excludes zero product distance, and then imposes D_paste=D_sign=0 on K_w(P), making the denominator in c_w(P) zero and the claimed collapse undefined.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Even ignoring the zero-denominator problem, the proof route is just substituting zero channels into the definition, with no substantive theorem-level content.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the c_w(P) definition by a primitive, non-gap-defined object and add a §9 exhibit that computes c_w(P), Gamma_w(P), h_H, and h_C from the same finite-support two-shock primitives.
Separate the hypotheses for strict separation from the boundary/equality theorem; do not assume positive product distance when proving a zero-channel collapse.
Anchor each flagship comparator to a section, proposition, equation, or quoted gap in PMW, Read, Drautzburg-Wright, GMR, LMS, and Montiel-Olea-Plagborg-Moller-Qian.
Fix the bibliography metadata for Wolf 2022, Drautzburg-Wright 2023, and Hoesch-Lee-Mesters 2024 before resubmission.

recommended_next_step: Do not send to Stage 0; redraft the kernel around a valid common-restriction feasible witness and a non-circular c_w(P) computation.
